title: Aureolaria rupestris
taxonomy: Aureolaria rupestris is a species of flowering plant in the family Orobanchaceae. It is native to North America and is a parasitic plant that obtains its nutrients by connecting to the roots of other plants. Aureolaria rupestris is often found growing in rocky areas and dry slopes, where it can form extensive colonies. The plant's classification is further divided into the genera and species levels, with it being a part of the genus Aureolaria. Aureolaria rupestris is a type of twining, hairy-stemmed plant that is also known as rock bell or rocky rockbell.

intro: Aureolaria rupestris, also known as rock bellwort, is a perennial plant species native to the mountainous regions of North America, characterized by its yellow, bell-shaped flowers and hairy, oval-shaped leaves. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which may aid in the treatment of wounds, skin irritations, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Aureolaria rupestris include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and phenylethanoid glycosides, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Traditionally, rock bellwort is used in herbal preparations such as tinctures, teas, and salves, often in combination with other plants, to support immune function and promote wound healing.
